_about: WS-1 (Output-Quality loop) held-out eval set. >=24 cases across >=7 subspecialties, each with the must_cover (operative facets a strong dossier should surface) / red_flags (cross-subspecialty content that must NOT contaminate this case) schema. `split` is `tune` (prompt/code iteration may read these) vs `eval` (held-out; read only by eval/quality_gate.py and the nightly judges). Partition ~1/3 tune ÷ 2/3 eval, disjoint by id. ids mirror eval/case_dictations.json.

cases.case_query: C5-6 ACDF for cervical spondylotic myelopathy with right C6 radiculopathy
must_cover: Recurrent laryngeal nerve at risk from tracheoesophageal retraction; counsel on transient hoarseness/dysphagia and consider approach side
Carotid sheath and carotid artery are the lateral limit; trachea and esophagus retracted medially
Esophageal/pharyngeal perforation risk with retractor blade placement
Spinal cord and exiting C6 nerve root at risk during discectomy/posterior osteophyte and PLL removal; intraoperative SSEP/MEP monitoring
Vertebral artery injury if dissection or uncovertebral/foraminotomy drilling is carried too far laterally
Durotomy/CSF leak (higher with OPLL or ossified PLL); have repair/patch and lumbar drain plan
Endplate preparation, interbody graft/cage sizing, anterior plating, and restoration of segmental lordosis
C5 nerve root palsy as a recognized post-decompression deficit
Expanding postoperative neck hematoma causing airway compromise — emergent bedside wound evacuation and reintubation as rescue
red_flags: Cranial nerve VII/VIII monitoring or intracranial aneurysm clipping
Awake cortical language or motor mapping

cases.case_query: L4-5 transforaminal lumbar interbody fusion for grade I degenerative spondylolisthesis with right L5 radiculopathy
must_cover: Exiting L4 and traversing L5 nerve roots at risk during facetectomy, cage insertion, and Kambin's triangle work
Dural tear/CSF leak with decompression — primary repair, fibrin patch, and flat bed rest plan
Pedicle screw trajectory with medial-wall breach risk to the thecal sac and lateral breach near the segmental vessels; consider navigation or intraoperative imaging
Reduction of the spondylolisthesis and restoration of disc height/foraminal volume and lumbar lordosis
Adequate central and foraminal decompression of the symptomatic right L5 root
Interbody graft/cage sizing and endplate preparation to avoid subsidence
Blood loss from epidural venous plexus and bone bleeding — hemostasis plan
Pseudarthrosis/hardware failure counseling and bone-health/fusion optimization
Positioning on a Wilson/Jackson frame with abdominal decompression and eye/pressure-point protection
red_flags: Cranial nerve or skull base monitoring

cases.case_query: Left L5-S1 microdiscectomy for a paracentral disc herniation with S1 radiculopathy
must_cover: Traversing S1 nerve root and thecal sac retraction during the paracentral discectomy
Correct-level confirmation with intraoperative fluoroscopy to avoid wrong-level surgery
Dural tear/CSF leak risk and primary repair plan
Adequate fragment removal versus aggressive disc-space evacuation and recurrent herniation risk
Preservation of the facet joint to avoid iatrogenic instability
Epidural venous bleeding control
Wound/discitis infection risk and closure
Positioning prone with abdominal decompression and pressure-point padding

cases.case_query: T6 laminectomy for resection of a left-sided intradural extramedullary thoracic meningioma
must_cover: Spinal cord at risk — intraoperative SSEP/MEP monitoring and avoidance of cord manipulation/retraction at the thoracic watershed
Correct-level localization in the thoracic spine (rib/vertebral counting, intraoperative imaging)
Watertight dural closure after the intradural resection to prevent CSF leak/pseudomeningocele
Dentate ligament division and gentle cord rotation for ventral/ventrolateral tumor access
Preservation of segmental radicular arteries (artery of Adamkiewicz territory) to avoid cord ischemia
Dural-tail/attachment management and en bloc versus internal-debulking strategy
Postoperative kyphosis/instability consideration if extensive laminectomy — fusion contingency
Positioning prone with the chest supported and venous pressure minimized
red_flags: Recurrent laryngeal nerve or anterior cervical approach content

cases.case_query: Left 2.5 cm vestibular schwannoma, retrosigmoid resection with hearing preservation
must_cover: Facial nerve (CN VII) identification and preservation, usually displaced over the anterior tumor surface; continuous intraoperative facial EMG and direct stimulation mapping
Cochlear nerve (CN VIII) and hearing preservation with intraoperative ABR/BAER monitoring
Trigeminal nerve (CN V) at the superior tumor pole
Lower cranial nerves (IX, X, XI) at the caudal pole
AICA and its loop/branches adherent to the capsule — preserve to avoid brainstem/cerebellar infarct
Internal auditory canal drilling with preservation of the labyrinth/posterior semicircular canal to protect hearing
Brainstem compression and cerebellar retraction injury
CSF leak through opened mastoid/petrous air cells — bone wax sealing and watertight closure; pseudomeningocele
Positioning (lateral/park-bench vs semi-sitting) and venous air embolism precautions if sitting
red_flags: Carotid endarterectomy or cervical carotid content
Awake language or motor cortex mapping
Lumbar disc or peripheral nerve content

cases.case_query: Right petroclival meningioma, retrosigmoid approach for resection with cranial nerve preservation
must_cover: Trigeminal nerve (CN V) draped over the tumor and at the superior pole
Facial/vestibulocochlear complex (CN VII/VIII) at the porus and along the petrous face — EMG and ABR monitoring
Brainstem and its perforators — sharp arachnoid dissection, no blind capsule traction, to avoid infarct
Basilar artery, AICA, and SCA encasement and preservation
Lower cranial nerves (IX, X, XI, XII) at the caudal/clival pole and swallow/airway implications
Petrous bone/air cell drilling and venous sinus (sigmoid/superior petrosal) handling
Subtotal resection strategy when the brainstem/cranial-nerve interface is adherent; adjuvant radiosurgery for residual
Watertight dural and air-cell closure to prevent CSF leak; venous air embolism precautions

cases.case_query: Endoscopic endonasal transsphenoidal resection of a nonfunctioning pituitary macroadenoma with suprasellar extension
must_cover: Internal carotid arteries forming the lateral sellar limit — Doppler/navigation and avoidance of lateral over-dissection
Optic chiasm and optic nerves superiorly — decompression goal and avoidance of upward traction injury
Cavernous sinus and cranial nerves III, IV, V1/V2, VI invasion/proximity
Intraoperative and postoperative CSF leak — graded skull-base reconstruction with a nasoseptal flap and lumbar drain contingency
Diabetes insipidus and anterior pituitary/hormonal axis management perioperatively
Extent-of-resection versus residual in the cavernous sinus; preserve normal gland and stalk
Sphenoid sinus anatomy, septations, and accurate midline/sellar floor localization
Postoperative epistaxis and sinonasal morbidity counseling

cases.case_query: Left CPA epidermoid cyst, retrosigmoid approach for microsurgical resection
must_cover: Cranial nerves V, VII, VIII (and lower CN IX-XI) encased/draped by pearly capsule — careful capsule dissection off the nerves with EMG/ABR monitoring
Brainstem and perforator preservation with sharp arachnoid technique
AICA and its branches woven through the cyst contents
Aseptic (chemical) meningitis risk from keratin spillage — copious irrigation and limiting subarachnoid contamination
Decision for near-total versus capsule-sparing resection where the capsule is densely adherent to neurovascular structures
Wide arachnoid exposure and cyst content removal across cisterns
Watertight dural/air-cell closure to prevent CSF leak and pseudomeningocele
Positioning and venous air embolism precautions

cases.case_query: Pterional craniotomy for clipping of ruptured left MCA bifurcation aneurysm
must_cover: Proximal control of ICA/M1 established before final neck dissection; temporary clips ready
Wide Sylvian fissure split with preservation of M1 and M2 branches
Lenticulostriate perforator preservation off M1
Aneurysm neck dissection and clip placement without parent-vessel or perforator compromise
Intraoperative rupture plan — temporary proximal clip, two suckers, then clip the neck
ICG videoangiography and/or micro-Doppler to confirm clip position and downstream vessel patency
Brain relaxation — basal cistern/lamina terminalis CSF drainage, mannitol, ventriculostomy
Adenosine-induced transient flow arrest as a rescue for a difficult or prematurely ruptured neck
Post-SAH cerebral vasospasm risk with nimodipine; optic nerve/chiasm proximity at the skull base
red_flags: Cranial nerve VII/VIII or lower cranial nerve monitoring
Oncologic resection margins or tumor pathology grading

cases.case_query: Right pterional craniotomy for clipping of a ruptured anterior communicating artery aneurysm
must_cover: Bilateral A1 proximal control and identification of both A2s and the AComA complex
Recurrent artery of Heubner and hypothalamic/perforating branches preservation
Gyrus rectus resection for exposure when needed and avoidance of frontal lobe injury
Aneurysm dome projection (anterior/posterior/superior/inferior) dictating dissection and clip strategy
Intraoperative rupture plan with temporary clipping and adenosine flow arrest contingency
ICG videoangiography to confirm A2 patency and complete neck obliteration
Brain relaxation via basal cistern/lamina terminalis CSF drainage and ventriculostomy
Post-SAH vasospasm surveillance and nimodipine; hyponatremia/cerebral salt wasting monitoring

cases.case_query: Left pterional craniotomy for clipping of a left posterior communicating artery aneurysm with third nerve palsy
must_cover: Internal carotid artery proximal control in the neck or intracranially before dissection
Oculomotor nerve (CN III) adjacent to the aneurysm — counsel on palsy and potential for recovery after clipping
Anterior choroidal artery and PComA/perforator preservation off the ICA
Aneurysm neck dissection and clip placement without ICA stenosis or perforator occlusion
Intraoperative rupture plan with temporary ICA clipping
ICG videoangiography/micro-Doppler to confirm ICA and branch patency
Brain relaxation and basal cistern CSF drainage
Post-SAH vasospasm risk and nimodipine if ruptured

cases.case_query: Resection of a right parietal Spetzler-Martin grade II arteriovenous malformation
must_cover: Circumferential dissection identifying and controlling feeding arteries before the draining vein
Preservation of the main draining vein until the nidus is devascularized to avoid intraoperative rupture
Deep white-matter perforating feeders as the hazardous last step — meticulous hemostasis
Functional cortex proximity (sensorimotor) and neuromonitoring/navigation to protect eloquent tissue
Normal perfusion pressure breakthrough hemorrhage risk and blood-pressure control postoperatively
Preoperative embolization to reduce flow through deep or inaccessible feeders
Intraoperative or postoperative angiography to confirm complete nidus obliteration
Brain relaxation and meticulous staged hemostasis throughout
red_flags: CSF diversion for hydrocephalus as the primary aim

cases.case_query: Endovascular coil embolization of a ruptured anterior communicating artery aneurysm
must_cover: Femoral/radial arterial access and triaxial guide/microcatheter navigation to the AComA complex
Working-angle angiography and 3D rotational runs to define neck and dome before coiling
Coil framing, filling, and finishing with attention to A2 patency and neck remnant
Thromboembolic complication risk — heparinization and rescue intra-arterial thrombolysis/antiplatelet plan
Intraprocedural rupture/perforation plan — reverse heparin, continue coiling/balloon, ventriculostomy
Balloon-/stent-assisted technique decision in a ruptured aneurysm and the antiplatelet implications
Radiation/contrast dose stewardship and contrast nephropathy precautions
Post-SAH vasospasm surveillance, nimodipine, and EVD for hydrocephalus
Access-site hematoma/retroperitoneal bleed monitoring

cases.case_query: Stent-assisted coil embolization of a basilar apex aneurysm
must_cover: Vertebrobasilar access and navigation to the basilar apex with a triaxial system
Posterior cerebral artery and superior cerebellar artery origin preservation at the apex
Thalamoperforator preservation — avoid coil/stent compromise of perforators off the apex
Stent-assisted technique with dual antiplatelet therapy and its hemorrhage trade-off
Thromboembolic risk with heparinization and rescue antiplatelet/thrombolysis plan
Intraprocedural perforation plan and reversal
Working projections to define the neck and protect both P1 origins
Radiation/contrast stewardship and access-site monitoring

cases.case_query: Flow-diverter stent placement for a large unruptured right cavernous/paraophthalmic internal carotid artery aneurysm
must_cover: Dual antiplatelet therapy with platelet-function testing before and after flow-diverter placement
Accurate device sizing and wall apposition across the aneurysm neck along the ICA
Ophthalmic artery and perforator/side-branch coverage and the risk of delayed occlusion
Delayed aneurysm thrombosis with progressive occlusion over months and mass-effect/perforator considerations
In-stent thrombosis and thromboembolic stroke risk — heparinization intraprocedurally
Delayed rupture risk of large aneurysms after flow diversion and blood-pressure considerations
Triaxial access and careful navigation across the ICA siphon
Radiation/contrast stewardship and access-site monitoring

cases.case_query: Mechanical thrombectomy for an acute left middle cerebral artery M1 occlusion (large vessel occlusion stroke)
must_cover: Time-to-reperfusion priority and confirmation of large-vessel occlusion on CTA/perfusion with salvageable penumbra
Triaxial access to the M1 occlusion and choice of stent-retriever and/or aspiration (first-pass effect)
Distal embolization to new territories and the use of a balloon-guide catheter for flow arrest
Vessel perforation/dissection and intracranial hemorrhage risk — reperfusion hemorrhage management
Number of passes and when to abandon to avoid endothelial injury
Blood-pressure management before and after reperfusion
Groin/access-site complications and closure
Post-procedure NIHSS monitoring and imaging for hemorrhagic transformation

cases.case_query: Awake craniotomy for left dominant frontal glioma with language and motor mapping
must_cover: Cortical language mapping with stimulation (speech arrest, naming/anomia, Broca's area) before and during resection
Subcortical white-matter tract mapping — arcuate/superior longitudinal fasciculus and IFOF
Primary motor cortex and corticospinal tract stimulation/monitoring
Asleep-awake-asleep anesthesia with scalp block and airway/LMA contingency
Stimulation-induced intraoperative seizure — iced saline/Ringer's irrigation as rescue, with after-discharge monitoring
Functional (negative-mapping) resection margins prioritizing language/motor preservation over maximal extent
MCA/Sylvian branch and cortical vein preservation
Neuronavigation with awareness of brain shift; intraoperative ultrasound or imaging adjunct
Patient cooperation/anxiety management and SMA syndrome counseling if SMA involved
red_flags: Posterior fossa / CPA cranial nerve monitoring
Skull base petrous/internal auditory canal drilling

cases.case_query: Bilateral subthalamic nucleus deep brain stimulation for Parkinson's disease
must_cover: Stereotactic frame/frameless targeting of the STN with MRI-CT fusion and indirect/direct coordinates
Microelectrode recording and/or awake macrostimulation testing for motor benefit and side-effect thresholds
Avoidance of a sulcal/ventricular trajectory to reduce hemorrhage risk along the cannula path
Intracerebral hemorrhage as the principal serious complication — trajectory planning and blood-pressure control
Stimulation-induced side effects (capsular contractions, paresthesias, mood/oculomotor) guiding lead placement
Intraoperative awake assessment of rigidity/tremor with the patient off medication
Lead anchoring, imaging verification, and staged pulse-generator implantation
Infection and hardware-erosion risk for the implanted system
red_flags: Watertight dural closure for CSF leak as the main concern

cases.case_query: Left anterior temporal lobectomy with amygdalohippocampectomy for mesial temporal lobe epilepsy
must_cover: Language dominance and memory assessment (Wada/fMRI) given the dominant-hemisphere resection
Extent of the anterior temporal/lateral neocortical resection limit to protect language cortex
Amygdalohippocampectomy with preservation of the optic radiation (Meyer's loop) to limit visual-field deficit
Middle cerebral artery branches and the anterior choroidal artery in the medial dissection
Brainstem/third nerve and the tentorial edge medially during the mesial resection
Intraoperative electrocorticography or prior intracranial monitoring informing resection margins
Seizure-freedom goal versus neuropsychological risk counseling
Vein of Labbe preservation to avoid venous infarction

cases.case_query: Right frontal non-eloquent convexity meningioma resection, Simpson grade I
must_cover: Circumferential dural devascularization and Simpson grade I resection (tumor plus dural attachment plus abnormal bone)
Middle meningeal/external carotid feeding vessels; consider preoperative embolization for a hypervascular tumor
Cortical draining vein preservation along the tumor margin
Anticipated blood loss — type and cross, large-bore IV access
Peritumoral edema management with dexamethasone and gentle brain handling
Internal debulking followed by capsule dissection off the pial plane (piecemeal vs en bloc)
Dural reconstruction/graft and cranioplasty of involved bone with watertight closure to prevent CSF leak
Seizure prophylaxis and early postoperative imaging for residual tumor or hematoma
red_flags: Awake cortical language mapping (lesion is non-eloquent)

cases.case_query: Right temporal craniotomy for resection of a glioblastoma with 5-ALA fluorescence guidance
must_cover: Maximal safe resection of the contrast-enhancing tumor with 5-ALA fluorescence and neuronavigation
Proximity to the optic radiation/Meyer's loop and the internal capsule — protect to limit field cut/hemiparesis
Middle cerebral artery branches and deep perforators at the medial/insular margin
Brain shift during resection degrading navigation — intraoperative ultrasound/imaging adjunct
Peritumoral edema and mass effect — dexamethasone and gentle handling
Molecular/pathologic tissue sampling (IDH, MGMT) and adjuvant chemoradiation planning
Postoperative hematoma in the resection cavity and early imaging
Seizure prophylaxis and venous/sinus preservation at the temporal base

cases.case_query: Suboccipital craniotomy for resection of a right cerebellar hemispheric metastasis with mass effect
must_cover: Obstructive hydrocephalus from fourth-ventricle/aqueduct compression — perioperative EVD/CSF diversion plan
Brainstem and fourth-ventricle floor proximity at the deep medial margin
Dural venous sinuses (transverse/sigmoid) at the craniotomy edges and the risk of venous air embolism
PICA and cerebellar perforator preservation
En bloc resection of the metastasis where feasible to reduce recurrence and seeding
Cerebellar swelling/tonsillar herniation risk and posterior fossa pressure management
Watertight dural closure to prevent CSF leak/pseudomeningocele
Adjuvant radiosurgery/whole-brain planning and systemic oncologic staging

cases.case_query: Transcortical resection of a left lateral ventricle (trigone) intraventricular tumor
must_cover: Transcortical versus interhemispheric-transcallosal trajectory choice and eloquent-cortex avoidance
Optic radiation and the trigone's relationship to visual pathways — field-deficit risk
Choroidal arteries (anterior/lateral posterior choroidal) feeding the tumor — early vascular control
Internal cerebral veins/deep venous system and the thalamostriate vein preservation
Intraventricular hemorrhage and the risk of postoperative hydrocephalus — EVD plan
Neuronavigation/tubular retractor to minimize white-matter injury on approach
CSF dynamics and avoidance of trapped ventricle
Seizure prophylaxis and early postoperative imaging

cases.case_query: Suboccipital craniotomy for resection of pediatric fourth-ventricle medulloblastoma
must_cover: Hydrocephalus management — preoperative EVD or ETV and a postoperative CSF diversion plan
Telovelar approach (rather than transvermian) to avoid vermian splitting and reduce cerebellar mutism/posterior fossa syndrome
Fourth ventricle floor and brainstem with facial colliculus preservation
Lower cranial nerves (IX, X, XII) at the floor — swallow/airway function and risk of postoperative bulbar dysfunction
PICA and tonsillar/brainstem perforator preservation
Watertight dural closure to prevent CSF leak and pseudomeningocele
Prone positioning, weight-based blood loss accounting, and venous air embolism risk in a child
Oncologic staging — postoperative MRI within 48 hours plus neuraxis MRI and CSF cytology for leptomeningeal spread
red_flags: Carotid endarterectomy or cervical vascular content
Lumbar disc or anterior cervical fusion content

cases.case_query: Suboccipital craniotomy for resection of a pediatric cerebellar pilocytic astrocytoma
must_cover: Gross-total resection goal for a pilocytic astrocytoma (often curative) balanced against cerebellar function
Obstructive hydrocephalus management with perioperative CSF diversion (EVD/ETV) planning
Cerebellar peduncle and dentate nucleus preservation to limit ataxia/dysmetria
Fourth-ventricle floor/brainstem proximity for tumors extending medially
Cerebellar mutism/posterior fossa syndrome counseling and approach selection
Dural venous sinus and PICA preservation; venous air embolism precautions
Watertight dural closure to prevent CSF leak/pseudomeningocele
Weight-based blood-loss accounting and pediatric positioning

cases.case_query: Resection of a pediatric suprasellar craniopharyngioma
must_cover: Hypothalamic injury risk — avoid aggressive dissection off the hypothalamus; weigh gross-total versus planned subtotal plus radiotherapy
Optic chiasm and optic nerve decompression and preservation of vision
Pituitary stalk and endocrine axes — diabetes insipidus and panhypopituitarism management perioperatively
Internal carotid arteries and their perforators in the suprasellar cisterns
Approach selection (endonasal endoscopic versus transcranial) by tumor extent and chiasm position
Cystic fluid (machinery-oil) spillage and chemical meningitis considerations
Hydrocephalus and CSF diversion planning
Postoperative weight/appetite/temperature dysregulation and hormone replacement

cases.case_query: Neonatal repair/closure of a lumbosacral myelomeningocele
must_cover: Neural placode reconstruction — reconstitute the neural tube and untether without injuring functioning neural tissue
Watertight dural closure to prevent CSF leak and meningitis
Layered closure (dura, fascia, skin) with attention to wound tension and skin flap viability
Associated hydrocephalus/Chiari II — plan for CSF diversion (VP shunt/ETV) timing
Avoidance/limitation of latex exposure given latex-allergy risk in spina bifida
Preservation of remaining motor/sensory and bowel-bladder function
Early closure timing to reduce infection risk in the neonate
Neonatal thermoregulation, fluid, and blood-loss considerations
red_flags: Recurrent laryngeal nerve risk in anterior cervical approach
